text: PurposeThis study aimed to investigate the influence of 24-h ambulatory blood pressure (BP) on cognitive function and neuropathological biomarkers in patients with Alzheimer's disease (AD) at the stages of mild cognitive impairment (MCI) and dementia.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: MethodsThe patients with AD were divided into the MCI (AD-MCI) group and the dementia (AD-D) group.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: Notably, 24-h BP variables, including BP level, coefficient of variation (CV) of BP, and pulse pressure, were collected and compared between the two groups.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The correlations between 24-h BP variables and the scores of cognitive domains were analyzed.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The independent influencing factors of cognitive domains of patients with AD were investigated.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The levels of neuropathological biomarkers of AD, including beta amyloid (A beta)(1-42), phosphorylated tau (P-tau), and total tau (T-tau), in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) were measured and compared between the two groups, and the correlations between 24-h BP variables and the levels of neuropathological biomarkers of AD were analyzed.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: ResultsDaytime CV of systolic BP (SBP) was significantly increased in the AD-D group compared to that in the AD-MCI group.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The 24-h and daytime CV of SBP and ambulatory pulse pressure were significantly and negatively correlated with memory score.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The average 24-h and average daytime SBP level and CV of SBP, daytime CV of diastolic BP (DBP), and 24-h, daytime, and night-time ambulatory pulse pressure were significantly and negatively correlated with language score.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The average 24-h SBP level, daytime CV of SBP, and 24-h, daytime, and night-time ambulatory pulse pressure were significantly and negatively correlated with attention score.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: Further analysis indicated that daytime CV of SBP as well as age and course of disease were the independent influencing factors of language.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: Age was also the independent influencing factor of memory and attention of patients with AD.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: T-tau level in CSF in the AD-D group was significantly higher than that in the AD-MCI group, but the levels of A beta(1-42), P-tau, and T-tau in CSF were not correlated with 24-h ambulatory BP variables.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: ConclusionDaytime CV of SBP was the independent influencing factor of language in patients with AD.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: The AD-D patients had significantly severe neurodegeneration than AD-MCI patients, which was, however, not through the influence of 24-h ambulatory BP variables on neuropathological biomarkers of AD.

meta.source_title: The Influence of 24-h Ambulatory Blood Pressure on Cognitive Function and Neuropathological Biomarker in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease.

text: Background Cerebrospinal fluid biomarker profiles characterized by decreased amyloid-beta peptide levels and increased total and phosphorylated tau levels at threonine 181 (pT181) are currently used to discriminate between Alzheimer's disease and other neurodegenerative diseases.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: However, these changes are not entirely specific to Alzheimer's disease, and it is noteworthy that other phosphorylated isoforms of tau, possibly more specific for the disease process, have been described in the brain parenchyma of patients.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: The precise detection of these isoforms in biological fluids remains however a challenge.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: Methods In the present study, we used the latest quantitative mass spectrometry approach, which achieves a sensitive detection in cerebrospinal fluid biomarker of two phosphorylated tau isoforms, pT181 and pT217, and first analyzed a cohort of probable Alzheimer's disease patients and patients with other neurological disorders, including tauopathies, and a set of cognitively normal controls.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: We then checked the validity of our results on a second cohort comprising cognitively normal individuals and patients with mild cognitive impairments and AD stratified in terms of their amyloid status based on PiB-PET imaging methods.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: Results In the first cohort, pT217 but not pT181 differentiated between Alzheimer's disease patients and those with other neurodegenerative diseases and control subjects much more specificity and sensitivity than pT181.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: T217 phosphorylation was increased by 6.0-fold in patients with Alzheimer's disease whereas T181 phosphorylation was only increased by 1.3-fold, when compared with control subjects.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: These results were confirmed in the case of a second cohort, in which the pT217 cerebrospinal fluid levels marked out amyloid-positive patients with a sensitivity and a specificity of more than 90% (AUC 0.961; CI 0.874 to 0.995).

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: The pT217 concentrations were also highly correlated with the PiB-PET values (correlation coefficient 0.72; P < 0.001).

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: Conclusions Increased cerebrospinal fluid pT217 levels, more than those of pT181, are highly specific biomarkers for detecting both the preclinical and advanced forms of Alzheimer's disease.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: This finding should greatly improve the diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease, along with the correlations found to exist between pT217 levels and PiB-PET data.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: It also suggests that pT217 is a promising potential target for therapeutic applications and that a link exists between amyloid and tau pathology.

meta.source_title: Cerebrospinal fluid phospho-tau T217 outperforms T181 as a biomarker for the differential diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease and PET amyloid-positive patient identification.

text: Myelination of projection neurons by oligodendrocytes is key to optimize action potential conduction over long distances.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

text: Whether FSI myelination contributes to the fine-tuning of intracortical networks is unknown.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

meta.source_title: Myelination of parvalbumin interneurons shapes the function of cortical sensory inhibitory circuits.

text: Cuprizone is a copper-chelating agent that induces pathology similar to that within some multiple sclerosis (MS) lesions.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: The reliability and reproducibility of cuprizone for inducing demyelinating disease pathology depends on the animals ingesting consistent doses of cuprizone.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Cuprizone-containing pelleted feed is a convenient way of delivering cuprizone, but the efficacy of these pellets at inducing demyelination has been questioned.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: This study compared the degree of demyelinating disease pathology between mice fed cuprizone delivered in pellets to mice fed a powdered cuprizone formulation at an early 3 week demyelinating timepoint.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Within rostral corpus callosum, cuprizone pellets were more effective than cuprizone powder at increasing astrogliosis, microglial activation, DNA damage, and decreasing the density of mature oligodendrocytes.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: However, cuprizone powder demonstrated greater protein nitration relative to controls.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Furthermore, mice fed control powder had significantly fewer mature oligodendrocytes than those fed control pellets.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: In caudal corpus callosum, cuprizone pellets performed better than cuprizone powder relative to controls at increasing astrogliosis, microglial activation, protein nitration, DNA damage, tissue swelling, and reducing the density of mature oligodendrocytes.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Importantly, only cuprizone pellets induced detectable demyelination compared to controls.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: The two feeds had similar effects on oligodendrocyte precursor cell (OPC) dynamics.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Taken together, these data suggest that demyelinating disease pathology is modelled more effectively with cuprizone pellets than powder at 3 weeks.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: Combined with the added convenience, cuprizone pellets are a suitable choice for inducing early demyelinating disease pathology.

meta.source_title: Cuprizone feed formulation influences the extent of demyelinating disease pathology.

text: There is increasing interest in the development of multiple sclerosis (MS) biomarkers that reflect central nervous system tissue injury to determine prognosis.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: We aimed to assess the prognostic value of kinesin superfamily motor protein KIF5A in MS by measuring levels of KIF5A in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) combined with analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs; rs12368653 and rs703842) located within a MS susceptibility gene locus at chromosome 12q13-14 region.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay was used to measure KIF5A in CSF obtained from two independent biobanks comprising non-inflammatory neurological disease controls (NINDC), clinically isolated syndrome (CIS) and MS cases.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: CSF KIF5A expression was significantly elevated in progressive MS cases compared with NINDCs, CIS and relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS).

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: In addition, levels of KIF5A positively correlated with change in MS disease severity scores (EDSS, MSSS and ARMSSS), in RRMS patients who had documented disease progression at 2-year clinical follow-up.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: Copies of adenine risk alleles (AG/AA; rs12368653 and rs703842) corresponded with a higher proportion of individuals in relapse at the time of lumbar puncture (LP), higher use of disease-modifying therapies post LP and shorter MS duration.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: Our study suggests that CSF KIF5A has potential as a predictive biomarker in MS and further studies into the potential prognostic value of analysing MS susceptibility SNPs should be considered.

meta.source_title: KIF5A and the contribution of susceptibility genotypes as a predictive biomarker for multiple sclerosis.

text: Multiple sclerosis (MS) features extensive connectivity changes, but how structural and functional connectivity relate, and whether this relation could be a useful biomarker for cognitive impairment in MS is unclear.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: This study included 79 MS patients and 40 healthy controls (HCs).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Patients were classified as cognitively impaired (CI) or cognitively preserved (CP).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Structural connectivity was determined using diffusion MRI and functional connectivity using resting-state magnetoencephalography (MEG) data (theta, alpha1, and alpha2 bands).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Structure-function coupling was assessed by correlating modalities, and further explored in frequency bands that significantly correlated with whole-brain structural connectivity.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Functional correlates of short- and long-range structural connections (based on tract length) were then specifically assessed.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Receiving operating curve analyses were performed on coupling values to identify biomarker potential.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Only the theta band showed significant correlations between whole-brain structural and functional connectivity (rho = -0.26, p = 0.023, only in MS).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Long-range structure-function coupling was stronger in CI patients compared to HCs (p = 0.005).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Short-range coupling showed no group differences.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Structure-function coupling was not a significant classifier of cognitive impairment for any tract length (short-range area under the curve (AUC) = 0.498, p = 0.976, long-range AUC = 0.611, p = 0.095).

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Long-range structure-function coupling was stronger in CI MS compared to HCs, but more research is needed to further explore this measure as biomarkers in MS.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Author Summary Cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis (MS) is common and relates to structural and functional connectivity.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: However, it remains unclear whether the interplay (coupling) between structural and functional connectivity could be a biomarker of MS-related cognitive impairment.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: This study investigated the cognitive relevance of structure-function coupling in 79 MS patients and 40 healthy controls using diffusion MRI and magnetoencephalography.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Results show that coupling was stronger in cognitively impaired MS patients compared to controls, but only when considering long-distance connections.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Nonetheless, classifier analyses indicated only weak biomarker potential in terms of sensitivity and specificity.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Future studies should include additional operationalization of coupling as well as longitudinal and regional or network level data.

meta.source_title: Structure-function coupling as a correlate and potential biomarker of cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis.

text: Johansson et al.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: describe plasma biomarkers in a longitudinal Swedish cohort of patients with monogenic Alzheimer's disease.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Plasma GFAP began to increase 10 years before symptom onset, prior to P-tau181 and NfL, suggesting that GFAP is mirroring Alzheimer's disease pathology upstream of tau phosphorylation and neurodegeneration.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Emerging plasma biomarkers of Alzheimer's disease might be non-invasive tools to trace early Alzheimer's disease-related abnormalities such as the accumulation of amyloid-beta peptides, neurofibrillary tau tangles, glial activation and neurodegeneration.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: It is, however, unclear which pathological processes in the CNS can be adequately detected by peripheral measurements and whether plasma biomarkers are equally applicable in both clinical and preclinical phases.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Here we aimed to explore the timing and performance of plasma biomarkers in mutation carriers compared to non-carriers in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Samples (n = 164) from mutation carriers (n = 33) and non-carriers (n = 42) in a Swedish cohort of autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease (APP p.KM670/671NL, APP p.E693G and PSEN1 p.H163Y) were included in explorative longitudinal analyses.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Plasma phosphorylated tau (P-tau181), total tau (T-tau), neurofilament light chain (NfL) and glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP) concentrations were measured with a single-molecule array method as previously described.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Plasma biomarkers were additionally correlated to Alzheimer's disease core biomarkers in the CSF.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Results from the longitudinal analyses confirmed that plasma P-tau181, NfL and GFAP concentrations were higher in mutation carriers compared to non-carriers.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: This change was observed in the presymptomatic phase and detectable first as an increase in GFAP approximately 10 years before estimated symptom onset, followed by increased levels of P-tau181 and NfL closer to expected onset.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Plasma P-tau181 levels were correlated to levels of P-tau181 and T-tau in the CSF.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Altogether, plasma P-tau181, GFAP and NfL seem to be feasible biomarkers to detect different Alzheimer's disease-related pathologies already in presymptomatic individuals.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Interestingly, changes in plasma GFAP concentrations were detected prior to P-tau181 and NfL.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Our results suggest that plasma GFAP might reflect Alzheimer's disease pathology upstream to accumulation of tangles and neurodegeneration.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: The implications of these findings need additional validation, in particular because of the limited sample size.

meta.source_title: Plasma biomarker profiles in autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease.

text: Background Alzheimer disease (AD) is the most common cause of dementia.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Preclinical AD is the period during which early AD brain changes are present but cognitive symptoms have not yet manifest.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: The presence of AD brain changes can be ascertained by molecular biomarkers obtained via imaging and lumbar puncture.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: However, the use of these methods is limited by cost, acceptability, and availability.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: The preclinical stage of AD may have a subtle functional signature, which can impact complex behaviours such as driving.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: The objective of the present study was to evaluate the ability of in-vehicle GPS data loggers to distinguish cognitively normal older drivers with preclinical AD from those without preclinical AD using machine learning methods.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Methods We followed naturalistic driving in cognitively normal older drivers for 1 year with a commercial in-vehicle GPS data logger.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: The cohort included n = 64 individuals with and n = 75 without preclinical AD, as determined by cerebrospinal fluid biomarkers.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Four Random Forest (RF) models were trained to detect preclinical AD.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: RF Gini index was used to identify the strongest predictors of preclinical AD.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Results The F1 score of the RF models for identifying preclinical AD was 0.85 using APOE epsilon 4 status and age only, 0.82 using GPS-based driving indicators only, 0.88 using age and driving indicators, and 0.91 using age, APOE epsilon 4 status, and driving.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: The area under the receiver operating curve for the final model was 0.96.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Conclusion The findings suggest that GPS driving may serve as an effective and accurate digital biomarker for identifying preclinical AD among older adults.

meta.source_title: GPS driving: a digital biomarker for preclinical Alzheimer disease.

text: Background: The hippocampus, entorhinal cortex (EC), and basal forebrain (BF) are among the earliest regions affected by Alzheimer's disease (AD) pathology.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: They play an essential role in spatial pattern separation, a process critical for accurate discrimination between similar locations.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Objective: We examined differences in spatial pattern separation performance between older adults with amnestic mild cognitive impairment (aMCI) with AD versus those with non-Alzheimer's pathologic change (non-AD) and interrelations between volumes of the hippocampal, EC subregions and BF nuclei projecting to these subregions (medial septal nuclei and vertical limb of the diagonal band of Broca - Ch1-2 nuclei) with respect to performance.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Methods: Hundred and eighteen older adults were recruited from the Czech Brain Aging Study.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Participants with AD aMCI (n = 37), non-AD aMCI (n = 26), mild AD dementia (n = 26), and cognitively normal older adults (CN; n = 29) underwent spatial pattern separation testing, cognitive assessment and brain magnetic resonance imaging.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Results: The AD aMCI group had less accurate spatial pattern separation performance than the non-AD aMCI (p = 0.039) and CN (p < 0.001) groups.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: The AD aMCI and non-AD groups did not differ in other cognitive tests.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Decreased BF Ch1-2 volume was indirectly associated with worse performance through reduced hippocampal tail volume and reduced posteromedial EC and hippocampal tail or body volumes operating in serial.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Conclusion: The study demonstrates that spatial pattern separation testing differentiates AD biomarker positive and negative older adults with aMCI and provides evidence that BF Ch1-2 nuclei influence spatial pattern separation through the posteromedial EC and the posterior hippocampus.

meta.source_title: Spatial Pattern Separation Testing Differentiates Alzheimer's Disease Biomarker-Positive and Biomarker-Negative Older Adults With Amnestic Mild Cognitive Impairment.

text: Background: Retinal thickness can be measured non-invasively with optical coherence tomography (OCT) and may offer compelling potential as a biomarker for Alzheimer's disease (AD).

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Retinal thinning is hypothesized to be a result of retrograde atrophy and/or parallel neurodegenerative processes.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Changes in the visual pathway are of particular interest in posterior cortical atrophy (PCA), the most common atypical AD phenotype predominantly affecting the parietal-occipital cortices.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: We therefore evaluated retinal thickness as non-invasive biomarker of neurodegeneration in well-characterized participants with posterior cortical atrophy (PCA) and typical Alzheimer's disease (tAD).

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Methods: Retinal thickness measures were acquired from 48 patient participants (N = 25 PCA; N = 23 tAD) fulfilling consensus diagnostic criteria and 70 age-matched controls.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Participants were recruited between 2014 and 2016.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: All participants underwent optical coherence tomography (OCT) imaging, including measurement of peripapillary retinal nerve fiber layer (pRNFL) thickness and total macular thickness (mRT).

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Participants did not show evidence of any significant ophthalmological conditions.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Subgroup analyses were performed in participants with available MRI and CSF measures, providing evidence of neurodegeneration and underlying AD pathology respectively.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Results: There was no evidence of overall between-group differences in pRNFL thickness (mean PCA 98.7 +/- 12.2; tAD 99.9 +/- 8.7; controls 99.6 +/- 10.0 mu m, one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) p = 0.92) or total mRT (mean PCA 266.9 +/- 16.3; tAD 267.8 +/- 13.6; controls 269.3 +/- 13.6 mu m, one-way ANOVA p = 0.75).

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Similarly, subgroup analysis with MRI biomarkers (PCA = 18, tAD = 17, controls = 31) showing neurodegeneration, and CSF biomarkers (PCA = 18, tAD = 14, controls = 13) supporting underlying AD pathology did not provide evidence of overall between-group differences in pRNFL or mRT measures (all p > 0.3).

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Conclusions: Retinal thickness did not discriminate tAD and PCA from controls or from one another despite unequivocal differences on standard clinical, neuro-imaging and CSF measures.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: Findings from this well-characterized sample, including cases with PCA, do not support the hypothesis that retinal neurodegeneration, measured using conventional OCT, is a useful biomarker for AD or PCA.

meta.source_title: Retinal thickness as potential biomarker in posterior cortical atrophy and typical Alzheimer's disease.

text: BackgroundGrowing evidence has demonstrated that DNA methylation (DNAm) plays an important role in Alzheimer's disease (AD) and that DNAm differences can be detected in the blood of AD subjects.

text: Most studies have correlated blood DNAm with the clinical diagnosis of AD in living individuals.

text: However, as the pathophysiological process of AD can begin many years before the onset of clinical symptoms, there is often disagreement between neuropathology in the brain and clinical phenotypes.

text: Therefore, blood DNAm associated with AD neuropathology, rather than with clinical data, would provide more relevant information on AD pathogenesis.MethodsWe performed a comprehensive analysis to identify blood DNAm associated with cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) pathological biomarkers for AD.

text: Our study included matched samples of whole blood DNA methylation, CSF A beta(42), phosphorylated tau(181) (pTau(181)), and total tau (tTau) biomarkers data, measured on the same subjects and at the same clinical visits from a total of 202 subjects (123 CN or cognitively normal, 79 AD) in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) cohort.

meta.source_title: Distinct CSF biomarker-associated DNA methylation in Alzheimer's disease and cognitively normal subjects

text: To validate our findings, we also examined the association between premortem blood DNAm and postmortem brain neuropathology measured on a group of 69 subjects in the London dataset.ResultsWe identified a number of novel associations between blood DNAm and CSF biomarkers, demonstrating that changes in pathological processes in the CSF are reflected in the blood epigenome.

meta.source_title: Distinct CSF biomarker-associated DNA methylation in Alzheimer's disease and cognitively normal subjects

text: Overall, the CSF biomarker-associated DNAm is relatively distinct in CN and AD subjects, highlighting the importance of analyzing omics data measured on cognitively normal subjects (which includes preclinical AD subjects) to identify diagnostic biomarkers, and considering disease stages in the development and testing of AD treatment strategies.

meta.source_title: Distinct CSF biomarker-associated DNA methylation in Alzheimer's disease and cognitively normal subjects

text: Moreover, our analysis revealed biological processes associated with early brain impairment relevant to AD are marked by DNAm in the blood, and blood DNAm at several CpGs in the DMR on HOXA5 gene are associated with pTau(181) in the CSF, as well as tau-pathology and DNAm in the brain, nominating DNAm at this locus as a promising candidate AD biomarker.ConclusionsOur study provides a valuable resource for future mechanistic and biomarker studies of DNAm in AD.

text: Objective This study was mainly conducted to explore the expression changes of GSDMD and conventional markers (including T-Tau, Tau181p, and A beta(1-42)) in the cerebrospinal fluid among patients with Alzheimer's disease (AD) and vascular dementia (VD), followed by determination of role of GSDMD in diagnosing and identifying AD and VD.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Methods In this study, 60 patients with VD, 60 patients with AD, and 50 healthy controls were enrolled.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Lumbar puncture was performed to collect cerebrospinal fluid samples.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Patients with VD and patients with AD were evaluated using the Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) scale, Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) scale, Clinical Dementia Rating (CDR) scale, Activity of Daily Living (ADL) scale, and Neuropsychiatric Inventory (NPI) questionnaire, aiming to determine the behavioral ability of patients.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: ELISA kit was purchased to determine the levels of GSDMD, T-Tau, Tau181p, and A beta(1-42) in cerebrospinal fluid, and the expression of inflammatory factors, IL-1 beta and IL-6, was also detected.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Results (1) The levels of GSDMD, T-Tau, and Tau181p in the cerebrospinal fluid were higher in patients with AD than those of patients with VD and healthy controls, while the levels of A beta(1-42) in the cerebrospinal fluid were lower in patients with AD than that in healthy controls and patients with VD.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: (2) GSDMD had good diagnostic accuracy in AD.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Additionally, GSDMD, T-Tau, Tau181p, and A beta(1-42) had good discrimination accuracy in distinguishing AD and VD.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: (3) The expression levels of inflammatory factors (IL-1 beta and IL-6) in cerebrospinal fluid were higher in patients with AD than those of healthy controls and patients with VD, which were positively correlated with GSDMD expression.

meta.source_title: Pyroptosis executive protein GSDMD as a biomarker for diagnosis and identification of Alzheimer's disease.

text: Conclusion The expression of GSDMD was increased in patients with AD, which could be used as a biomarker for AD diagnosis and identification from VD.

text: Material and methods We used 24 male Wistar rats that had received surgical lesions to the corpus callosum and were injected with lysolecithin.

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

text: Rats were divided into three groups: Group 1 included eight rats with lesions to the corpus callosum but not lysolecithin injections (sham group), group 2 included eight rats with lesions to the corpus callosum that were injected with lysolecithin (lysolecithin group), and group 3 included eight rats with lesions that were injected with lysolecithin and received VitD (VitD group).

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

text: We analyzed neurogenesis both in the subventricular zone and at the lesion site.

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

text: Results Administration of VitD promotes the proliferation and differentiation of neural stem cells in the subventricular zone and the migration of these cells to the lesion site in the corpus callosum; these cells subsequently differentiate into oligodendrocyte lineage cells and produce myelin basic protein.

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

text: This phenomenon was not caused by microglial activation, which was less marked in rats receiving VitD.

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

meta.source_title: Vitamin D increases remyelination by promoting oligodendrocyte lineage differentiation.

text: Conclusion Our results support the hypothesis that regardless of the presence of VitD deficiency, treatment with VitD may contribute to remyelination by promoting the proliferation of oligodendrocyte precursor cells.

text: Life-long myelination is promoted by neuronal activity and is essential for neural network plasticity and learning.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: OPCs are known to contact synapses and it is proposed that neuronal synaptic activity in turn regulates their behavior.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: To examine this in the adult, we performed unilateral injection of the synaptic blocker botulinum neurotoxin A (BoNT/A) into the hippocampus of adult mice.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: We confirm BoNT/A cleaves SNAP-25 in the CA1 are of the hippocampus, which has been proven to block neurotransmission.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: Notably, BoNT/A significantly decreased OPC density and caused their shrinkage, as determined by immunolabeling for the OPC marker NG2.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: Furthermore, BoNT/A resulted in an overall decrease in the number of OPC processes, as well as a decrease in their lengths and branching frequency.

meta.source_title: Synaptic silencing affects the density and complexity of oligodendrocyte precursor cells in the adult mouse hippocampus

text: These data indicate that synaptic activity is important for maintaining adult OPC numbers and cellular integrity, which is relevant to pathophysiological scenarios characterized by dysregulation of synaptic activity, such as age-related cognitive decline, Multiple Sclerosis and Alzheimer's disease.

text: Oligodendrocytes, myelin-forming cells in the brain, are vulnerable to oxidative stress.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Recent work indicates that air pollution causes demyelinating diseases such as multiple sclerosis.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: However, little is known about the mechanism of toxicity of ultrafine particulate matters (PMs) to oligodendrocytes.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Here, we aimed to determine whether oligodendrocyte precursor cells (OPCs) and mature oligodendrocytes (mOLs) are more vulnerable to ultrafine urban PMs (uf-UPs) than other types of brain cells and damage to adult OPCs and mOLs in the mouse brain exposed to uf-UPs.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: For in vitro experiments, following exposure to various concentrations (2, 20, and 200 mu g/mL) of uf-UPs, we measured survival rates, the amount of reactive oxygen species (ROS), and the total antioxidant capacities (TACs) of brain cells isolated from neonatal Sprague-Dawley rats.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: For animal experiments, after a four-week exposure to a uf-UP suspension (20 mu L, 0.4 mg/mL), we enumerated the number of damaged cells and typed damaged cells in the white matter of the cerebellum of uf-UP-exposed mice.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: MTT assays and Hoechst staining demonstrated that OPCs and mOLs were more vulnerable to uf-UP-induced damage than astrocytes and cortical neurons at 2, 20, and 200 mu g/mL of uf-UPs examined in this study (p < 0.05).

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Damage to OPCs and mOLs depended on uf-UP concentration.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: DCF assays and DHE staining indicated that the amount of ROS generated in OPCs and mOLs was significantly higher than in other brain cell types (p < 0.05).

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: In contrast, TAC values in OPCs and mOLs were significantly lower than those of other brain cell types (p < 0.05).

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Fluoro-Jade B (FJB)-positive cells in the cerebellar white matter of the uf-UP-exposed group were significantly greater in number relative to the control group.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Double immunofluorescence indicated that FJB-positive cells are NG2-positive adult OPCs and carbon anhydrase II-positive mOLs.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: Taken together, our findings suggest that oxidative stress induced by uf-UPs in the brain impairs adult OPCs and mOLs, causing demyelination and reducing the capacity for remyelination.

meta.source_title: High Vulnerability of Oligodendrocytes to Oxidative Stress Induced by Ultrafine Urban Particles.

text: MicroRNAs (miRNAs) are important regulators of several biological processes, such as cell growth, cell proliferation, embryonic development, tissue differentiation, and apoptosis.

meta.source_title: Mitochondrial MicroRNAs in Aging and Neurodegenerative Diseases.

meta.source_title: Effect and Mechanism of Catalpol on Remyelination via Regulation of the NOTCH1 Signaling Pathway.

meta.source_title: Effect and Mechanism of Catalpol on Remyelination via Regulation of the NOTCH1 Signaling Pathway.
